content.full_text:
June 17, 1991

Ms. Susan Bobrow, Attorney
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

Thank you for the assistance provided to me on May 14, 1991
regarding my consulting services to recyclers and processors. I
inadvertently failed to mail my previous letter, which necessitates
sending this letter to you.

As we discussed, I formerly worked for the Department of
Conservation as an Associate Management Auditor. In that capacity
I audited recyclers, processors and distributors. I did not have
"decision making authority" as defined by the Fair Political
Practices Act.

I ceased working for the Department of Conservation (DOC) on March
31, 1991 and began working for the Department of Social Services
(DSS) on April 1, 1991. My consulting business did not begin until
the middle of April 1991. As I stated to you, my clientele does
not and will not include any entity that I have audited. In
addition, I do not represent any of my clients to the Department of
Conservation. I only provide consultation services in the area of
interpreting DOC's regulations.

It is my understanding, according to the information I presented to
you and reintegrated above, that you concluded that my activities
do not present a conflict of interest.

As you suggested, I contacted Margie Nagae, DSS - Labor Relations
(322-7487), regarding my consulting services and she stated that my
consulting activities do not present a conflict of interest with my
new job as a General Auditor III in the Community Care Licensing
Division.

If you have any questions or concerns regarding the information
noted above, please contact me at your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,

Joyce A. Richardson
7832 Casa Bella Way
Citrus Heights, California 95610
(916) 726-7411
